<!-- Generated by a 25-agent geometric-consistency / LEGO functional-correctness workflow, 2026-06-15. -->

# Vibemesh-AI — Geometric Consistency & Functional Correctness (LEGO axle-vs-tube)

## 1. Executive summary

**Root cause.** Generated parts are self-colliding because the generator reasons about each feature *independently against the solid it cuts*, never against the *other features it crosses*. A part is composed as `union(solids)` then `difference(cutters)`, and nothing in the contract told the model that a cutter must not destroy a structural/functional feature it passes through. OpenSCAD happily emits a manifold solid in which an axle bore has gutted the very clutch tubes that make a brick grip — manifold, but non-functional and physically illogical, exactly the user's "the hole is cutting these pipes."

**Yes, the injected kit exemplar TAUGHT it.** At committed HEAD, `server/exemplars.mjs` modeled the collision verbatim: full-height **solid** anti-stud cylinders placed on the **same stud lattice as the studs** (`grid(nx,ny)` → `(i+0.5)*pitch`), and two horizontal axle bores at `azc=axle_d/2+1=3` running the full width — the bore band sits inside every tube's z-span and the nearest tube-to-bore center distance is `pitch/2 = 4.0mm < bore_r(2.15)+tube_r(2.35) = 4.5mm`. Compile-measured, the bore removes real clutch-tube material (multiple verifiers got 3–26 mm³ depending on params/$fn — magnitude varies, the slice is unambiguous). The model copied the worked anti-example and amplified it.

**Single highest-leverage fix:** the corrected, compile-verified KIT exemplar in `server/exemplars.mjs` — it deconflicts by construction (hollow annular tubes on the inter-stud lattice, bores routed on stud columns, any tube in a bore's path locally skipped, tubes unioned back **after** the hollow cut). It is the few-shot for every kit generation, so fixing it moves output more than any prose. **Both the exemplar rewrite and the prompt's new "Geometric consistency and functional integrity" section already exist in the working tree, uncommitted (`git status` = `M server/exemplars.mjs`, `M server/prompt.mjs`).** The dominant remaining action is to **commit them** and add a deterministic regression guard so the fix can never silently revert — today's bench scores the gutted-tube part a perfect `buildability=1.0` with zero notes.

---

## 2. How real LEGO does it

The system the user invoked is fully documented (corroborated across cailliau.org, bartneck.de, zoeblade.com, brickowl, BrickNerd):

| Dimension | Value |
|---|---|
| Stud pitch | 8.0 mm |
| Stud diameter | 4.8 mm (height 1.8) |
| Plate height / brick height | 3.2 / 9.6 mm |
| Wall thickness | ~1.2–1.6 mm |
| Anti-stud tube | OD ~6.5 mm / ID ~4.8–5.0 mm (≈0.75–0.8 mm wall) |
| Technic axle/pin hole | Ø ~4.8–4.9 mm, **center 5.8 mm above the brick floor** |

Two conventions resolve the hole-vs-tube conflict:

1. **Tubes live on the inter-stud (dual) lattice, offset half a pitch.** Each Ø6.5 tube sits in the gap between four studs and grips them on its *outer* wall (4 diagonal studs at center-distance √(4²+4²)=5.657 mm; stud_r+tube_inner_r=4.8 < 5.657 → contact). This is the 1958 Kirk Christiansen stud-and-tube principle. **Putting a tube coaxial with a stud is not how LEGO clutches** — the exemplar's original bug.

2. **The cross-hole sits on a stud column and is held HIGH (5.8 mm).** The hole runs along the stud columns — i.e. *between* the inter-stud tubes — and is raised to 5.8 mm so a full stud still clears beneath it.

**The corridor/relief principle (load-bearing nuance).** Routing on a stud column is *necessary but not sufficient* at real dimensions: a full Ø6.5 tube (r=3.25) plus a Ø4.3 bore (r=2.15) need 5.4 mm of separation but only have pitch/2 = 4.0 mm. A full tube and an axle bore **cannot coexist in the same 8 mm bay.** Real Technic bricks therefore **remove the round tube in the hole row** and substitute ridge/"asymmetric-teeth" anti-studs. Generalized: *when two swept volumes can't both fit, separate them OR relieve one* — choose a clear corridor, and where the bore must still cross, locally delete the obstacle and reinforce the opening. Height also helps (a high hole clears low obstacles), but height alone never deconflicts a full-height tube.

---

## 3. Root-cause analysis (file:line)

**A. Exemplar bug (the teacher).** Committed HEAD `server/exemplars.mjs`:
- `module anti_stud() cylinder(d=stud_d+stud_fit, h=body_h);` — full-height **solid** post, not a tube.
- `grid(nx,ny) anti_stud();` and baseplate `grid(nx,ny) stud();` — tubes and studs on the **same** `(i+0.5)*pitch` lattice.
- `for (x=[pitch, bx-pitch]) translate([x,0,azc]) axle_bore();` with `azc = axle_d/2 + 1 = 3` — bore band inside every tube's z-span; X-distance 4.0 < 4.5 → each bore slices two tubes. Compile-confirmed material removal.

**B. Missing interference / negative-space reasoning.** The whole contract knows only *one* clearance concept — 1-D **fit** clearance for a single joint (`server/prompt.mjs:99,105` "female = male + fit"). There was never a feature-vs-feature, 3-space "does this cutter destroy that structure" check. A grep for `collide|interfer|deconflict|corridor|relieve|clearance pocket|intersect structure` over `server/` returned zero real hits at HEAD.

**C. Prompt gap.** At HEAD the design-rules section and the final self-check (`server/prompt.mjs`, old self-check ended at item 8) had **no** non-collision rule. The model had a worked anti-example (the exemplar) and no counter-rule — the bug was over-determined.

**Why the bench never caught it.** `bench/buildability.mjs` scores part-enum presence, per-piece render, flatness, bed-fit, and connector **keywords** (`CONNECTOR_RE`) — no geometry intersection anywhere (`gate.mjs` NUMERIC = dimScore/iou/placementScore/buildabilityScore; categorical zero-tol = compiled/overSplit). The gutted exemplar scored `buildability=1.0`, `hardFail:false`, zero notes. `run.mjs` re-renders each `part` as one fused solid (`run.mjs:360`), so the intra-part damage is already baked in and invisible to bbox/minZ/IoU. This mirrors `docs/FIDELITY-DIAGNOSIS.md` Theme F.

---

## 4. The fix

### 4a. Corrected exemplar (working tree — `server/exemplars.mjs`, the key OpenSCAD)

```scad
bx = nx*pitch; by = ny*pitch;
azc = body_h/2;                          // axle-bore center height  (see note below: prefer 5.8 datum)
bore_cols = [pitch/2, bx - pitch/2];     // axles run on STUD columns
skip_r = axle_d/2 + spin_fit/2 + tube_od/2 + 0.6;   // tube-relief radius around each bore

// real LEGO tube: a hollow ANNULUS; its bore grips the stud, its outer wall clutches 4 studs
module anti_stud() difference() {
  cylinder(d=tube_od, h=body_h);
  translate([0,0,-1]) cylinder(d=stud_d+stud_fit, h=body_h+2);
}
module axle_bore() rotate([-90,0,0]) translate([0,0,-1]) cylinder(d=axle_d+spin_fit, h=by+2);

module stud_grid() for(i=[0:nx-1]) for(j=[0:ny-1])              // STUD lattice
  translate([(i+0.5)*pitch,(j+0.5)*pitch,0]) children();
module tube_grid() for(i=[1:nx-1]) for(j=[1:ny-1])             // INTER-STUD lattice, minus sliced nodes
  let(cx=i*pitch)
  if (min([for(b=bore_cols) abs(cx-b)]) >= skip_r)
    translate([cx, j*pitch, 0]) children();

module body() {
  difference() {
    cube([bx, by, body_h]);
    translate([wall, wall, -1]) cube([bx-2*wall, by-2*wall, body_h-wall+1]);  // hollow underside
    for (x=bore_cols) translate([x, 0, azc]) axle_bore();                     // 2 cross axle bores
  }
  // tubes added AFTER the hollow cut so they live inside the cavity, INTACT
  intersection() {
    translate([wall, wall, 0]) cube([bx-2*wall, by-2*wall, body_h]);
    tube_grid() anti_stud();
  }
}
```

Verified at defaults (nx6/ny4): `bore_cols=[4,44]` (stud columns); tube nodes `{8,16,24,32,40}` (inter-stud); cols 8 and 40 are within `skip_r=6.0` of a bore so they're skipped; kept cols 16/24/32 clear every bore by ≥6.6 mm. **Kept-tube ∩ bore = EMPTY (0 mm³)**; all 5 parts compile manifold flat on z=0; 9–15 intact annular clutch tubes survive.

**One correction to land before committing — the bore height.** The exemplar uses `azc = body_h/2 = 4.8`, not the real-LEGO 5.8 mm datum. This is a fidelity miss, *not* a functional defect (deconfliction is in X, height-independent), but a bare `azc=5.8` *also* breaks at short bodies (at `body_h=6`, 5.8+2.15 punches the roof). Ship a **clamped, datum-anchored** form:

```scad
hole_z = 5.8;  // [4:0.1:7]   real-LEGO Technic hole datum
azc = (body_h >= 9.0) ? hole_z : body_h/2;   // datum on full bricks, mid-height fallback on short bodies
// guard: keep azc in [stud_h + boreR + 0.2, body_h - wall - boreR - 0.2]
```

**Small-grid clutch caveat (verified):** the skip leaves zero tubes at nx2/ny2 and nx≤?/ny1, so a 2-wide/2x2 brick loses all clutch. Add a fallback (short corner posts on a free inter-stud node) or a one-line prompt note so thin bricks still grip.

### 4b. Prompt section (exact wording, already in `server/prompt.mjs:111–118` + self-check 9 at :146)

> **# Geometric consistency and functional integrity (mandatory)**
>
> A part is not done when it merely COMPILES — OpenSCAD will happily produce a manifold solid in which a cutter has gutted the very feature that makes the part work (a bore drilled straight through the clutch tubes, a weight pocket that breaks into a bearing seat, a screw hole that opens a wall it was meant to anchor). The geometry must be INTERNALLY CONSISTENT, FUNCTIONAL, and BUILDABLE AS DRAWN — like the real object it represents. Features are NOT placed independently; they must be deconflicted against each other.
>
> - A through-hole, bore, slot, or pocket must NOT destroy a structural or functional feature in its path. Before adding a cutter, ask what internal geometry (clutch tubes, ribs, bosses, bearing seats, axle channels, walls) lies along its swept volume, and route the cutter through a CLEAR CORRIDOR — or, where it must cross, LOCALLY RELIEVE the obstacle (skip that tube/rib, or widen a clean channel for the cutter) so the remaining functional features stay INTACT and reachable. A sliced-open tube, a half-eaten boss, or a bore with no clean bearing channel is a FAILED answer, even if it is manifold.
> - Position features on a deconflicted layout, not on top of each other. Mating/functional features … and the cutters that serve other functions … must occupy SEPARATE corridors of the part. Real LEGO is the worked example: clutch tubes sit on the inter-stud lattice (between studs); axle/pin cross-holes run along the stud columns, in the gaps BETWEEN tubes …
> - The same rule generalizes to ALL parts, not just LEGO. … a fidget spinner's outer bearing seat and its weight bores must each have their own pocket with solid material between them — a weight bore must not break into the bearing race or the center bore; a bracket's mounting holes must clear its internal ribs; a hinged box's pin channel must not open into the lid cavity. …
> - Build order matters … add it AFTER the hollowing cut (union it back in), and confine it with an intersection() to the cavity if needed …
>
> **Self-check 9:** No cutter destroys a structural or functional feature: every hole/bore/pocket has a clear corridor (or a deliberate local relief), every clutch tube / bearing seat / axle channel / rib it passes near survives INTACT … For a studded part, clutch tubes are on the inter-stud lattice and any cross-bore runs between them, slicing none.

**One clause to add** (real LEGO uses it and the fidget benefits): *"and set a cross-hole's height to clear features below it — real Technic holes sit ~5.8 mm up for exactly this reason."* Vertical deconfliction is currently absent from the rule.

### 4c. How it generalizes (the fidget and beyond)

The rule is part-agnostic and the prompt already names the non-LEGO cases. For the fidget spinner: the **center bearing seat**, the **satellite bearing pockets**, and the **rim weight bores** are all cutters into the arm/hub; a weight bore that breaks into the bearing race, or a bearing pocket that eats the arm root, is the *same* non-empty-intersection failure as the axle-vs-tube slice. The canonical move is identical: trace each cutter's swept volume, route it on a clear corridor, and where it must cross structure, locally relieve + reinforce so both survive.

---

## 5. How we'll PROVE it (interference / functional eval)

The fix is currently **unfalsifiable** by the bench. Three deterministic, library-free additions (reusing `bench/compare.mjs` `compileScad`/`parseStl`/`meshVolume`):

1. **Static exemplar fixture (ship now, zero-tolerance).** Add a gold/self-test that recompiles the body's tube material *with* and *without* the axle bores and asserts `vol(tubes) − vol(difference(tubes, bores)) ≈ 0` (use the volume-delta form, not raw `intersection()` — an empty intersection returns null STL, ambiguous with a compile failure). Verified discriminator: 0.000 mm³ on the corrected geometry, ~51 mm³ on a `skip_r=0` broken control. Costs no API call → fully deterministic → **tight tolerance** (not the live-API wide tolerance the findings' draft mistakenly copied). This directly ratchets the exemplar/prompt against regression.

2. **`_debug` debug-group enum contract (for arbitrary output).** To measure *generated* parts, the prompt must mandate a hidden `_debug = "off"; // [off, positives, negatives]` enum: `positives` renders ONLY the protected internal structure (tubes/bosses/bearing-seat walls — **never the pierced outer shell**, or every legitimate hole false-fails); `negatives` renders ONLY the cutters as solids. The eval drives both with `-D` and asserts `intersection(positives, negatives) ≈ 0`. Required: filter `_`-prefixed names out of the slider UI (`src/lib/params.ts:buildParam` — add `if (name.startsWith('_')) return null`; the parser does NOT already drop them) and place the enum under a `/* [Hidden] */` group. Treat an empty group (a part with no cutters) as PASS, not a render failure.

3. **T10-technic bench task + gate wiring.** Add a fresh kit task ("Technic-style brick, horizontal 4mm axle hole that must not cut the underside clutch tubes"), compute `interferenceVol` via the named-module / `_debug` probe, surface `interferenceScore` in `gate.mjs metricsOf`, ship it **advisory for one baseline cycle**, confirm the deconflicted exemplar scores 0, then flip to **zero-tolerance** (like `overSplit`). Do not tag single-solid tasks (T1/T3/T8). A T11-fidget sibling reuses the same probe once the named-module contract lands.

---

## 6. Ranked fix plan (impact ÷ effort, with regression guards)

| # | Action | Impact | Effort | Regression guard |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Commit** the working-tree exemplar + prompt fixes (`server/exemplars.mjs`, `server/prompt.mjs`). They're uncommitted — a checkout loses them. | High | Trivial | `npm run bench` (T7-kit) → `npm run bench:gate`; confirm compiled/overSplit green, buildability unchanged. |
| **2** | Correct `azc` to the clamped 5.8 datum + small-grid clutch fallback (4a). | Med | Small | Bore-clearance assertion across `body_h` slider extremes; verify ≥1 surviving tube per row. |
| **3** | Add clause: cross-hole height clears features below (5.8 mm rationale) to the prompt rule. | Med | Trivial | Prose-only, additive; inert for solids. |
| **4** | Static exemplar interference fixture, zero-tolerance gate entry (5.1). | High | Small–Med | Deterministic; tag kit/structured only, never T1/T3/T8. |
| **5** | `_debug` enum contract + `src/lib/params.ts` UI filter + `[Hidden]` placement (5.2). | High | Small | Filter `_`-names from UI **and** param counts (`run.mjs:countParams`, `buildability.mjs:numericParams`); empty group = PASS. |
| **6** | T10-technic task + `interferenceScore` in `gate.mjs`, advisory→zero-tol (5.3). | High | Med | One extra live-API gen/run; advisory one cycle before flipping; re-baseline once. |
| **7** | T11-fidget task (bearing seat + weight bores) reusing the probe. | Med | Med | Defer until the named-module contract (5.2) lands. |

Items 1–4 are the must-ship core (commit + correctness + a standing ratchet). 5–7 extend the guard to arbitrary output and the non-LEGO case.

---

## 7. Integration with `docs/FIDELITY-DIAGNOSIS.md`

This investigation is **complementary, not duplicative**: FIDELITY-DIAGNOSIS covers *visual* fidelity (symmetry, module distinctness — its proposed `bench/fidelity.mjs` and a new T10); this one covers *internal geometric consistency / functional correctness* (interference — a `bench/interference.mjs` / deconfliction probe). Both feed the **same** `gate.mjs` NUMERIC block and both require a `baseline.json` re-seed.

**Combined implementation order (single baseline reshuffle):**
1. Commit the deconfliction exemplar + prompt section (this report, item 1).
2. Land the FIDELITY prompt/exemplar changes (fidelity track).
3. Add both eval lanes together: `bench/interference.mjs` (deconfliction) + `bench/fidelity.mjs` (symmetry/distinctness), sharing copied/exported `voxelize`/`makeGrid` from `compare.mjs`.
4. Add the new bench tasks together (T10-technic for interference; the fidelity track's T10/symmetry task — **renumber to avoid a collision**, e.g. interference→T10, fidelity→T11).
5. Wire all new scores into `gate.mjs metricsOf`/NUMERIC **advisory** for one cycle.
6. Run `BENCH_SAMPLES=k npm run bench` once, confirm corrected exemplar scores clean on both lanes, then `npm run bench:gate --update-baseline` **once** and commit — a single combined baseline.json reshuffle, then flip the interference/overSplit-class metrics to zero-tolerance.

---

**Key file references:** `server/exemplars.mjs` (corrected KIT_EXEMPLAR — working tree, uncommitted), `server/prompt.mjs:111–118` + `:146` (geometric-consistency section + self-check 9 — working tree, uncommitted), `server/prompt.mjs:99,102,104,105` (connector/fit rules + LEGO lattice guidance), `server/providers.mjs` (kit exemplar injection), `src/lib/params.ts:buildParam` (needs `_`-name UI filter), `bench/buildability.mjs` (no interference check — the gap), `bench/gate.mjs` (NUMERIC/categorical gates), `bench/run.mjs:360` (per-part fused render — why damage is invisible), `bench/compare.mjs` (`compileScad`/`parseStl`/`meshVolume` — reuse for the probe), `docs/FIDELITY-DIAGNOSIS.md` (complementary fidelity track).
